pattern: Because ages span a small fixed range, bucketing counts by age and summing valid requester-recipient age pairs avoids comparing every individual pair.
hints: Two people with the same ages either both qualify or both do not, so group people by age and reason about age pairs, not person pairs.
Count how many people have each age, then for each ordered pair of ages decide once whether a request is allowed and multiply the two counts.

A request from age x to age y is allowed only when y is greater than half of x plus seven and y does not exceed x; for equal ages each person sends to all others of that age, so subtract the self-request.

edge_inputs.description: first example, two equal ages request each other
second example, consecutive ages above the threshold
third example, large gaps limit valid requests
single person can send to nobody
very young ages fall under the half-plus-seven cutoff
many identical adults all request one another
age exactly at the half-plus-seven boundary is excluded

stress.description: many people with ages drawn across the full legal range
stress.note: large n with a tiny value domain stresses the bucketed pair sum
